title: lichen_link_rx rx_ctx uses link_ctx.link_key pointer after potential cleanup
description:
File: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lichen_l2.c:375-385

The rx_ctx.link_key is set to link_ctx.link_key (line 379):
  .link_key = link_ctx.has_link_key ? link_ctx.link_key : NULL,

rx_mutex is held, but lichen_l2_enable(false) calls lichen_link_cleanup() which may zero link_ctx.link_key. The cleanup happens under both mutexes, so no race today.

However, the pointer is captured into rx_ctx before lichen_link_rx() runs. If lichen_link_rx() is slow or blocks, and we unlock rx_mutex then re-lock for cleanup (not current code, but plausible refactor), the pointer could dangle.

Risk: Maintainer moves cleanup outside mutex, or adds async cleanup, causing use-after-clear of key material.

Mitigation: Copy link_key bytes into rx_ctx (not just pointer), or document that cleanup MUST be serialized with rx_mutex.
